# vima

> Agent-first ticket tracker. Tickets are Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. All output is JSON.

## Quick start

```sh
vima help --json                    # full command schema (runtime-discoverable)
vima create "Title" -t task -p 2    # create a ticket
vima list --pluck id,title          # list open tickets (minimal fields)
vima show ID                        # full ticket detail
vima start ID                       # mark in_progress
vima close ID --reason "Done"       # close with reason
```

## Key commands

- `create "Title" [flags]` — create ticket. Flags: `-t type`, `-p priority`, `--dep ID`, `--tags a,b`, `--batch` for JSON stdin
- `list [filters]` — open tickets. Filters: `--tag`, `--type`, `--priority`, `--assignee`, `--status`
- `ready` — tickets with no open dependencies
- `blocked` — tickets with open dependencies
- `closed` — closed tickets
- `show ID` — single ticket detail
- `update ID [flags]` — update fields: `--title`, `--description`, `--priority`, `--tags`, `--status`, `--type`
- `start ID` — set status to in_progress
- `close ID [--reason]` — close ticket(s)
- `reopen ID` — reopen closed ticket
- `dep add ID DEP` — add dependency; `--blocks` reverses direction
- `dep tree ID` — show dependency tree
- `is-ready ID` — exit 0 if ready, 2 if blocked
- `add-note ID "text"` — add note (reads stdin if no text arg)

## Output control

- Default: NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
- `--pluck FIELD` — extract single field (bare value) or `--pluck a,b` (object)
- `--count` — integer count only
- `--full` — include heavy fields (description, notes, body) in list output
- `--pretty` — human-readable colored table

## Exit codes

- 0: success
- 1: general error (invalid_field, io_error, yaml_error, etc.)
- 2: cycle detected or ticket blocked
- 3: not found or ambiguous ID
- 4: conflict (id_exists)

## Error format (stderr)

```json
{"error":"not_found","message":"ticket not found: xyz","suggestion":"run `vima list --pluck id` to see available tickets"}
{"error":"ambiguous_id","message":"ambiguous id 'ab': matches ab-1234, ab-5678","matches":["ab-1234","ab-5678"],"suggestion":"use --exact or provide more characters of the ID"}
```

## Automation

- `VIMA_EXACT=1` or `--exact` — disable fuzzy ID matching
- `--batch` — create multiple tickets from JSON stdin with `$1`, `$2` back-references
- No interactive prompts — fully non-interactive by design
- JSON to stdout, errors to stderr — pipe-safe

## Distributed sync

Local multi-agent and multi-worktree use works out of the box (shared `.vima/`, file locking, optimistic concurrency). For distributed collaboration — remote teammates, cloud-hosted agents, CI systems, different machines — the `vima-sync` plugin adds git-based coordination:

```sh
vima sync start vi-1234 --assignee agent-a   # pull, claim, commit, push — one command
vima sync list --pluck id,title              # pull fresh state, no push
```

- Mutations (create, update, start, close, reopen, delete, batch): pull → execute → commit → push
- Read-only commands: pull → execute (no push)
- Push conflicts: automatic retry with semantic conflict detection (exit 6 if claimed, exit 5 if stale)
- Config: `VIMA_SYNC_REMOTE` (default: origin), `VIMA_SYNC_BRANCH` (default: current), `VIMA_SYNC_RETRIES` (default: 3)

## Storage

Tickets are `.vima/tickets/{id}.md` — Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Diffs and merges like code. vima walks up the directory tree to find `.vima/`, or use `VIMA_DIR` env var.
